prevalence.trend: stable; improved recognition with MRI availability

burden.mortalityRate: Low; indirect via disability and comorbidity

vulnerablePopulations: patients with delayed diagnosis in primary care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Inflammatory back pain with imaging (sacroiliitis on X-ray/MRI) and/or HLA-B27 plus clinical features; ASAS criteria; exclude mechanical back pain

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; biologic access and physiotherapy availability influence outcomes

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: MRI access variable in SUS; rheumatology referrals concentrated in capitals
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Exercise/physiotherapy, NSAIDs first-line; TNF/IL-17 inhibitors for refractory disease in specialized care; smoking cessation counseling
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Delayed diagnosis, uneven access to MRI and biologics, and limited rehab services outside major centers
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Specialist centers can provide biologics and multidisciplinary care in larger cities
